title: saxifraga serpyllifolia subsp. glutinosa
intro: Saxifraga serpyllifolia subsp. glutinosa, also known as sticky saxifrage, is a perennial plant species in the saxifrage family (Saxifragaceae) that is native to Europe, western Asia, and northern Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Saxifraga serpyllifolia subsp. glutinosa include its potential to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea or tincture, and has been traditionally used to treat a variety of ailments, including respiratory issues and digestive problems.
